expectations.text: ownership-gap-named: The draft frames this as an ownership gap — nobody is accountable — rather than as anyone's fault
expectations.evidence: 'no one owns this cross-team — it's fallen in the gap between us' — structural framing, no blame assigned to any individual or team.

expectations.text: timeline-urgency: The draft mentions the 3-week deadline or the first ship date to create appropriate urgency
expectations.evidence: 'Three teams (including mine) are shipping features in the next three weeks' — opening line establishes urgency with specific timeline.

expectations.text: prior-attempts-referenced: The draft references the #architecture-discuss discussions or the informal attempts to align
expectations.evidence: 'I've raised it twice in #architecture-discuss and both times the response was "good point, let's align" — but nothing has moved.'

expectations.text: asks-for-owner-not-solution: The ask is for someone to own the decision or coordinate — not for the recipient to personally solve the technical problem
expectations.evidence: 'What I need is either: (a) someone designated as the owner who can drive a decision... or (b) a clear decision that each team handles their own rate limiting independently' — asks for ownership/decision, not technical solution.
